$comment: Curated transcript of the agentic coding session(s) that built this app. Add another object to `sessions` to merge another chat. Each `message` has a one-line `summary` (always visible) and optional `content` (revealed on expand). Tool calls capture the agent's actions during a single response.

sessions.title: Earlier session — original brief
sessions.summary: Voice-to-text brief, follow-up decisions on LLM/hosting/scope, and the refined prompt that drove the rebuild.
sessions.note: Paste the content of https://claude.ai/code/session_01CoUTE9TPr2udPNrcqwgFss into this session and I'll convert it into the same shape. The duplicated original prompt will be deduped on merge.

sessions.title: Build session — Phases 1–4, deploy, polish
sessions.summary: Rebuild the data dictionary in a fresh repo, deploy to Railway, harden the LLM extraction pipeline.

messages.summary: Original voice-to-text brief + refined spec + decisions (no LLM key, decide hosting later, build Phase 1 now).
messages.content:
Voice-to-text request: build a polished demo data dictionary web app. Pull in dictionaries from around the world, let businesses define their own terms, search a word and see all available definitions, use NLP/LLM to consolidate definitions aligned with industry practice, allow inline prompts to refine, flag terms, browse defined terms in a searchable list. Also wants a roadmap and feature ideas to make it feel like a real app.

Follow-up decisions: no LLM key (use open-source); hosting decided later; build Phase 1 now.

Refined spec attached: FastAPI + SQLModel + SQLite backend with 25 seeded business terms; Next.js 15 + React 19 + Tailwind v4 frontend; single-container Railway deployment; concrete data model (Term, Definition, Source, ImportJob, LLMRefinement) with the FlagStatus / SourceType / ImportStatus enums; specific endpoint list; visual design requirements; a 4-phase ROADMAP.

messages.summary: Scaffolded repo, built FastAPI backend + Next.js frontend, wired single-container Docker deployment, pushed Phase 1.
messages.content: Created the repo structure on the claude/demo-app-roadmap-pPutU branch. Built a complete Phase 1 deliverable: backend with 9 SQLModel-based files, 4 routers, dictionary lookup adapter, 25-term seed; frontend with the App Router, shadcn-style custom components, dashboard / terms index / term detail / search pages; one-stage Dockerfile that builds the static export and serves it from FastAPI on a single port. Verified locally with curl smoke tests.

tool_calls.summary: Inspected empty repo state (git status, list)
Created .gitignore + README + ROADMAP
Wrote 12 backend files (models, schemas, routers, services, seed)
Built venv, installed deps, smoke-tested every endpoint
Wrote 25 frontend files (UI primitives, providers, sidebar, pages, lib/api+types)
npm install + next build → 506 prerendered pages
Wrote Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, railway.toml
End-to-end test with static export mounted → all routes 200
git add + commit + push -u origin claude/demo-app-roadmap-pPutU

messages.summary: Implement all the deferred phases now (Phase 2–4 + cross-cutting polish).
Shipped Phases 2–4 + polish: extra dictionary sources, LLM consolidation with heuristic fallback, import/export/PDF, audit log, tags, command palette, tour, animated UI.
messages.content: Big push (47 files, +5,384 / −439). Backend gained Wiktionary / Wikipedia / Standards adapters with caching, an LLM service with Ollama + HuggingFace + heuristic fallback, refine/import/export/PDF/audit/tags/demo-reset/system routers, AuditEvent + Tag + LLMRefinement models, IndustryContext enum. Frontend gained sonner toasts, cmdk command palette, custom guided tour, animated stat cards, /import (CSV mapping + JSON + PDF preview), /tags facet page, /t/[slug] public share, refine box, tag editor, audit timeline, bulk actions. Verified end-to-end backend then frontend build (533 prerendered pages) before pushing.

tool_calls.summary: Extended models: Tag/TermTag/AuditEvent/IndustryContext + LLMRefinement source/confidence
Wrote dictionary_service.py: Wiktionary + Wikipedia + Standards + cache + concurrent fan-out
Wrote llm_service.py: Ollama + HF + heuristic consolidate() with confidence scoring
Wrote pdf_extractor.py: PyMuPDF4LLM + 4 regex patterns
Wrote 5 new routers: refine, import_export (CSV/JSON/PDF + CSV/JSON/MD export), tags, audit, system (incl. demo reset)
Re-installed deps with pymupdf4llm; smoke-tested every new endpoint
Hit a 'MissingGreenlet' async-lazy-load bug in seeder; fixed by inserting TermTag rows directly
Built test PDF in pymupdf, ran preview + import → 3 of 4 patterns matched
Frontend: providers + sonner + cmdk command palette + keyboard shortcuts + tour + animated number
Frontend: refine box, tag editor, audit timeline, industry select, confidence badge, empty state
Frontend pages: /import (CSV mapping UI), /tags, /t/[slug] share, enhanced term detail
next build → 533 static pages; static-export wired into FastAPI mount
Updated README + ROADMAP, committed, pushed

messages.summary: Run the app.
Started uvicorn on :8080 with the static export mounted; gave URLs.

tool_calls.summary: Recreated venv, installed deps, npm install + build
Copied frontend/out → backend/app/static; started uvicorn 0.0.0.0:8080
Smoke-tested every page route + API endpoint

messages.summary: Got '{"detail":"Not Found"}'.
Diagnosed — `/api` and `/api/` had no handler, so the SPA catchall returned the API 404. Added a friendly JSON landing.

tool_calls.summary: Probed every URL path; found `/api`, `/api/` → 404 JSON
Added `@app.get('/api')` returning {app, version, docs, health} pointers

messages.summary: 8080 is running a different app — use a different port.
Killed mine, restarted on :8090, identified itself via /api landing.

tool_calls.summary: pkill uvicorn; start on :8090
Verified `/api` returns Data Dictionary v0.2.0

messages.summary: Still getting errors / can't see the app from the browser.
Realised the sandbox has no port forwarding to the user's browser. Explained the limitation and offered Railway / local-clone paths.
messages.content: Verifying via `curl 127.0.0.1:8090` only proves the process is listening *inside the sandbox*. The user's browser is on a different network with no tunnel between them. Confirmed by checking `ss -ltn`, hostname, env vars — there's no ngrok / cloudflared / tailscale installed and no platform-provided forwarding panel. Pivoted to: deploy to Railway, or clone + run locally.

messages.summary: The PR isn't working.
Diagnosed — the repo had only one branch and no `main`, so GitHub had no base to PR into. Created an empty `main` orphan branch.

tool_calls.summary: Confirmed no PRs exist (open, closed, or any state)
Confirmed only one branch exists
git switch --orphan main; commit --allow-empty; push -u origin main

messages.summary: Merge your branch into main.
Merged with --allow-unrelated-histories (the orphan main + feature branch had no common ancestor).

tool_calls.summary: git merge --allow-unrelated-histories --no-ff feature → main: 80 files, +10,485
git push origin main; switched back to feature branch

messages.summary: Railway error: Dockerfile invalid — flag '--mount=type=cache' is missing an id argument.
Removed the BuildKit cache mount + the syntax directive entirely (Railway's builder rejected it). Kept npm ci/install behavior.

tool_calls.summary: Dropped `# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7` and the `RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm` line
Rebased on remote (a PR had been merged on GitHub), pushed to both branches

messages.summary: Healthcheck failing — '1/1 replicas never became healthy'.
Three fixes: background the DB seed, drop railway.toml's startCommand (so the Dockerfile's `sh -c` shell handles ${PORT}), bump healthcheck timeout 30s → 120s.

tool_calls.summary: main.py: replaced sync seed with asyncio.create_task(_background_seed()) so /api/health is reachable in ~1s
railway.toml: removed startCommand; relies on Dockerfile CMD with sh -c to expand ${PORT}
railway.toml: healthcheckTimeout = 120
Pushed to feature branch; fast-forwarded main; pushed main

messages.summary: That worked — but the regex isn't finding terms well in PDFs. Would an LLM RAG approach work? Can you use Hugging Face?
Clarified it's not RAG, just LLM-based extraction. Added an LLM extractor (chunked + JSON-mode) with regex fallback, documented free HF token setup.
messages.content: Added `extract_terms()` in llm_service.py that chunks text into ~6k-char windows with 400-char overlap, prompts the LLM for STRICT JSON with a tight system prompt, and tolerates code fences / preamble / trailing commas in the response. PDF extractor now runs LLM first and merges in regex hits to cover anything LLM drops. When no LLM is configured the regex pass remains. Preview API returns an `extractor` label so the UI can show which path ran (`llm`, `llm+regex`, `regex`).

tool_calls.summary: llm_service.py: added EXTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, _chunk_text, _extract_json_array, _call_*_extract
pdf_extractor.py: extract_from_pdf_bytes_async() runs LLM + regex and merges
import_export router: switched to async path, surfaces `extractor` label
Schema: PDFExtraction.extractor field; frontend types + UI to show it
Unit-tested chunker + JSON parser on tricky cases
README: documented HF token setup at huggingface.co/settings/tokens

messages.summary: It had llm=true but still dropped back to regex.
Root cause: HF deprecated the old api-inference endpoint for instruct models in 2025. Added Groq backend, rewrote HF to use router.huggingface.co chat completions, surfaced LLM errors in the UI.
messages.content: Rewrote the LLM service around a single OpenAI-compatible helper. Three backends, preference order Groq → Ollama → HF. `_invoke()` records every failure reason. The PDF preview API surfaces the first 3 errors so the UI can show 'LLM extraction failed — fell back to regex. Cause: <exact HTTP error>'. Added Groq config (GROQ_API_KEY, GROQ_MODEL) and HF_PROVIDER. README documents all three with Groq as the recommended path.

tool_calls.summary: config.py: added GROQ_API_KEY/GROQ_MODEL/HF_PROVIDER; llm_enabled() includes Groq
llm_service.py rewrite: _call_openai_compat helper; Groq + HF + Ollama backends through one path
extract_terms returns errors; PDFExtraction.llm_errors surfaces them
Frontend: yellow banner in PDF preview showing the failure reason
/api/system shows groq/ollama/huggingface flags + active model label

messages.summary: How do I add Groq with a fallback to other LLM?
Already built — fallback chain is automatic per-call. Just set GROQ_API_KEY and HF_API_TOKEN; the code tries Groq first, falls through on failure.
Got a Groq token error.

Identified it as the daily token budget. Reduced per-PDF token usage ~75% (chunks 6k→10k chars, max_tokens 1400→800), added 429-aware blacklist so Groq drops out for the rest of the batch instead of being retried per chunk.

tool_calls.summary: Bumped _CHUNK_CHARS to 10000 + _CHUNK_OVERLAP to 500
extract_terms uses max_tokens=800 (down from 1400)
_invoke accepts skip:set; appends to skip on 429/'rate'/'quota'/'token' errors
extract_terms threads skip across chunks; bails if every backend skipped
README: 'Surviving Groq's free tier rate limits' section

messages.summary: Add a 'behind the scenes' tab showing how the app was built — three views (Apple Finder, Windows tree, conversation cards) with offset swimlanes and collapse-depth controls. Merge with the earlier chat URL.

Confirmed the sandbox proxy blocks claude.ai (403) so the earlier chat URL can't be auto-fetched. Built the feature now with this chat as the seed — the earlier session drops in as a second array entry once you paste it.

tool_calls.summary: Probed the URL: 503 'DNS cache overflow' from sandbox proxy; broader probe showed claude.ai/wikipedia/github all 403 (allowlist policy)
build_log.json: schema + curated session 2 + placeholder for session 1
Behind-the-scenes page + three viewers (cards w/ swimlanes, finder, tree)
